note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/ModularForms/Basic.lean:75 - structure ModularForm extends SlashInvariantForm Γ k, requiring holomorphicity (holo') and boundedness at cusps (bdd_at_cusps'); this is the standard definition of a modular form of weight k for a congruence subgroup Γ ≤ SL(2,ℤ).
verify_note: Decl confirmed in oracle + source at Basic.lean:75; structure literally bundles SlashInvariantForm + holomorphicity (holo') + boundedness at cusps (bdd_at_cusps'), which is exactly the definition of modular form; Q870797 label/description confirm concept match; exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/Order/PrimeSeparator.lean:44 - proves via Zorn's lemma that in a distributive lattice, any ideal disjoint from a filter extends to a prime ideal still disjoint from that filter; Boolean algebras are distributive lattices, so BPI is the specialization of this lattice-theoretic separator theorem, not a bespoke Boolean-algebra statement.
verify_note: Decl confirmed in oracle + source at PrimeSeparator.lean:44; taking F = trivial filter {top} recovers exactly the classical BPI statement (every proper ideal extends to a prime ideal), and no more specific Boolean-algebra-only BPI decl exists in Mathlib, so 'generalization' (not 'exact') is the honest label.
